interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y (12.68%) peaks highest. The 364D dip below 182D and the 2Y bulge suggest near-term liquidity stress and tight banking sector conditions. Yields trading above the 10% repo rate across all tenors reflect persistent inflationary expectations and BB's contractionary stance. The inverted 1Y-2Y segment signals market anxiety over near-term monetary tightening.
implications: Banking sector liquidity remains strained, with demand for short-to-medium term government borrowing pushing 2Y yields abnormally high relative to the mid-curve
Elevated inflation expectations persist beyond BB's comfort zone, keeping the entire curve above the policy repo rate and limiting room for near-term easing
The 15Y-20Y steepness and 2Y hump suggest fiscal supply pressure from government borrowing and uncertainty around medium-term debt sustainability
